about.title: About EastCulture
about.subtitle: A quiet online space to learn Tai Chi and traditional Chinese arts, step by step.
about.storyText1: EastCulture was created for people who love traditional Chinese culture but feel lost about where to start. Many students tell us they want to learn Tai Chi or Qigong, but local classes are far away, or the teaching style is too fast and hard to follow.
about.storyText2: Here, we slow everything down. We break movements into small, clear steps and explain the ideas behind them, so you can build a calm, safe practice at home—even in a small living room.
about.philosophy1: Slow and safe: we care more about your joints and breathing than about looking cool on video.
about.philosophy2: Rooted in tradition: movements are taught together with their cultural and philosophical background.
about.philosophy3: Clear structure: short lessons, visible progress, and a simple learning path from beginner to advanced.
about.philosophy4: Realistic practice: we design sequences that fit into busy modern life, not just ideal retreat schedules.
about.instructorText: Our main instructor has years of experience in Tai Chi, Qigong and traditional culture teaching for overseas students. The wider team includes people with backgrounds in psychology, education, and design, all helping to make the learning experience gentle, clear and visually calm.
about.design1: Each module focuses on one theme only: posture, breathing, stepping, or a short form.
about.design2: Most lessons are 10–20 minutes so you can repeat them easily.
about.design3: We use multiple camera angles and slow demonstrations whenever possible.
about.design4: Future updates will add more live sessions, practice plans and seasonal programs.
about.who1: Beginners who want to start Tai Chi without pressure.
about.who2: People who are busy, stressed, and need a calm daily ritual.
about.who3: Overseas Chinese and international students who miss a connection to Chinese culture.
about.who4: Anyone who likes the idea of moving meditation more than intense workouts.
about.faq1A: No. The beginner path is designed for complete newcomers. You only need a little space and comfortable clothing.
about.faq2Q: Do I need special shoes or uniform?
about.faq2A: At the beginning, no. Soft, flat shoes and loose clothes are enough. Later, if you wish, our shop offers simple Tai Chi clothing and swords.
about.faq3A: Yes, lessons are being prepared with English subtitles.
about.faq4A: No. All content is for wellness and cultural learning only. Please follow your doctor's advice for any medical condition.

fengShui.title: Feng Shui & Space Harmony: Let Your Home and Desk Support You
fengShui.subtitle: This course approaches Feng Shui in a practical way: no heavy formulas, no fear-based superstition. We return to the simple question: does this space help you feel calm, safe and focused? By adjusting directions, movement paths, light and clutter, we gently align your home and workspace with your intentions.
fengShui.section1Content: We start from how a person feels, not only from compass numbers: What is the first thing you see when you enter? Is your back supported when you sit? When you work, do you face a wall or an open view? These details influence your sense of possibility and motivation. We use simple before/after examples to show the impact of each change.
fengShui.section2Content: We don't ask you to renovate or move. Instead, the focus is on small corrections within your current space: adjusting desk direction, slightly shifting the bed, softening harsh door-to-door lines, improving light and adding living elements like plants.

topics.0: Basic directions and the idea of a 'qi mouth' of the home—how doors, windows and corridors influence movement and energy.
topics.1: Bedroom principles: bed position, headboard, mirrors and electronics arrangement for better rest and recovery.
topics.2: Desk & workspace layout: creating a sense of focus and control, reducing constant distractions and procrastination.
topics.3: Entrance, living room and storage: decluttering and creating visual anchors so coming home feels lighter and more welcoming.
topics.4: Gentle remedies for common Feng Shui worries (beams over the bed, door facing door, bathroom positions) without creating fear.

audience.0: People who feel their home is cluttered or chaotic but don't know where to start.
audience.1: Renters or flatmates who want small, affordable tweaks instead of big renovations.
audience.2: Freelancers and remote workers who spend many hours at home and want their space to support their career.
audience.3: Anyone curious about Feng Shui who wants a balanced, respectful yet down-to-earth introduction.

fengShui.disclaimer: Note: This program draws on traditional Feng Shui concepts and elements of environmental psychology, aimed at helping you make more mindful choices about your space. It is not real-estate, architectural or financial advice—please consult local professionals for major housing or renovation decisions.

tcm.title: Traditional Chinese Medicine for Daily Life: Meridians, Body Types & Everyday Care
tcm.subtitle: This course is not about turning you into a doctor. Instead, we use everyday language and stories to explain TCM ideas: What are meridians? What do cold, heat, deficiency and excess really mean? Why do some people handle late nights better than others? You will learn to read the signals of your own body and make kinder choices in rest, food and lifestyle.
tcm.section1Content: Instead of only focusing on the painful spot, TCM looks at the whole person: Is your qi and blood flowing? Are sleep, emotions and digestion in balance? Through diagrams and real-life examples we introduce the ideas of 'wholeness' and 'balance', and why the same symptom can come from very different patterns.
tcm.section2Content: The course is divided into short modules: basic concepts, case stories, mini self-checks and everyday tips. Each lesson is 8–15 minutes so you can easily relate the content to yourself or your family without memorising heavy terminology—just a few clear guiding ideas you can reuse.

modules.0: Meridians & Qi-Blood Basics: using simple 'water pipe' and 'road traffic' analogies to understand flow, blockage and regulation.
modules.1: Common body types: tendencies toward cold, heat, qi deficiency, blood deficiency and how each pattern reacts to food, weather and stress.
modules.2: Digestive health & the Spleen-Stomach system: why TCM calls it 'the root of post-heaven qi' and what that means in modern life.
modules.3: Emotions & the five organs: accessible explanations of ideas like 'the Liver governs free flow' so you can see how mood and body affect each other.
modules.4: Seasonal care: how to gently adjust lifestyle and habits across spring, summer, autumn and winter.

audience.0: People who often feel cold hands and feet, get 'heat' symptoms easily, or experience the same minor issues again and again.
audience.1: Office workers who want to care for themselves in a gentle, long-term way instead of only relying on painkillers and pushing through.
audience.2: Learners interested in TCM who tried reading books before but found them too abstract or complicated.
audience.3: Students of Tai Chi, qigong or other mind–body practices who want a clearer 'map' of the body's internal logic.

tcm.disclaimer: Important: This course is for sharing traditional TCM theory and lifestyle ideas only. It does not provide diagnosis or prescriptions. For any concrete illness or ongoing discomfort, please consult qualified medical professionals first.

lesson1.title: Wing Chun 10 Health Qigong Full Course
lesson1.subtitle: Structured Daily Gentle Training. Correct Bad Posture, Build Solid Core Strength & Boost All-Day Consistent Energy
lesson1.duration: Full Step-by-Step Lessons | ~7 Min Per Routine
lesson1.intro: You don't need long workouts or difficult moves. This Wing Chun Health Qigong – 10 Forms comes from authentic Wing Chun stances, breathing and meridian principles. No fighting, no jumping, no intense effort, zero injury. The full routine is only about 7 minutes, perfect for busy modern life. It relaxes your body, improves blood circulation, calms your mind, and helps you sleep better.

painPoints.0: Long hours working and phone use make your neck and shoulders stiff, with dizziness, numb hands, and a visible neck hump
painPoints.1: Lower back pain and tightness from sitting too long; you can't sit or stand long, bending down is hard
painPoints.2: Joints feel stiff and creaky — knees weak, wrists sore, fingers numb, movement not flexible
painPoints.3: Body feels tight and heavy, hard to relax, low flexibility, easy to feel sore all over
painPoints.4: Cold hands and feet all year, weak energy, easily tired, lack of strength
painPoints.5: Poor sleep: hard to fall asleep, light sleep, easy to wake up, still tired after sleeping
painPoints.6: Stress, anxiety, chest tightness, irritable, cannot relax even when resting
painPoints.7: Low immunity, easily tired and sick, body getting weaker
painPoints.8: Tried yoga, running, gym, massage — too tiring, hard to keep, or causes joint pressure
painPoints.9: Want to keep healthy but no time, no space, no foundation, don't know where to start

lesson2.title: Wing Chun Practical Self-Defense Full Course
lesson2.subtitle: Easy Real-Life Protection Techniques. Gain Solid Confidence, Stay Calm & Feel Safe In Any Situation
lesson2.duration: Beginner Friendly Guide | ~12 Min Per Lesson
lesson2.intro: You don't need strength, height, or long-term training. This Wing Chun Self-Defense 9 Forms comes from real combat principles. It uses skill over strength, controls angles, neutralizes attacks instantly, and works in real dangerous situations: street attacks, elevator traps, wrist grabs, bear hugs, front punches, and more. Simple, safe, highly effective — beginners can learn in minutes.

painPoints.0: Walking alone at night, taking elevators, feeling unsafe and anxious
painPoints.1: Meeting taller / stronger attackers, too weak to block and easy to get hurt
painPoints.2: Attacked with punches, grabs, or chokes — panicked with no idea what to do
painPoints.3: Trapped in small spaces (elevator, corridor, bus), nowhere to run
painPoints.4: Grabbed from behind, struggling makes it more dangerous
painPoints.5: Tried other martial arts: too hard, too tiring, too violent, not practical
painPoints.6: No martial arts experience, small body, worried you can't defend yourself
painPoints.7: Forced to fight back blindly, making the attacker more aggressive

0.name: Tan Sau + Step + Chain Punches
0.relieves: Right straight punch from a taller attacker
0.core: Deflect, not block; expose gaps; counter fast

1.name: Fu Sau + Punch
1.core: Change angle, seal power, push attacker off balance

2.name: Lap Sau vs. Swing Punch
2.core: Stick to the arm, change attack path, open gaps
2.benefit: Avoid being knocked out; counter quickly

3.name: Bong Sau + Roll + Hook Punch
3.relieves: Elevator attack, front assault, no room to retreat
3.core: Turn waist, seal the arm, close-quarters strike
3.benefit: Works in tight spaces; defend and counter at once

4.name: Double Slap Control
4.core: Dual slap to disable attack ability
4.benefit: Shut down all incoming moves in 1 second

5.name: Tan Sau + Step + Block & Slap
5.core: Step forward, block face, control distance
5.benefit: Keep attacker away and take control

6.name: Cross Finger Strike vs. Straight Punch
6.benefit: One move to weaken the attacker

7.name: Elbow Control & Escape
7.relieves: Wrist grab, clothes grab, held arm
7.benefit: Break free in 1 second even if grabbed tight

8.name: Horse Stance + Rear Strike
8.relieves: Bear hug from behind, sudden restraint
8.core: Stable stance, rear counter, break free
8.benefit: Life-saving move for women in real danger

0.title: Skill beats strength
0.desc: Women, seniors, and small builds can all use it

2.title: Works anywhere
2.desc: Elevator, street, corridor, no space needed

3.title: Safe & non-violent
3.desc: Deflect and control, not brutal fighting

5.title: Covers 90% dangers
5.desc: Punches, grabs, hugs, traps — all solved

audience.0: Women who walk alone or work late
audience.2: Students and people who use elevators often
audience.3: Parents who want to protect their family
audience.4: Anyone who wants practical, easy self-defense
audience.5: Beginners with no time or energy for hard training

gains.0: Stay calm when attacked — no panic, no blind fight
gains.1: Escape grabs, hugs, and punches in seconds
gains.2: Defend yourself even in small spaces
gains.3: Protect yourself with skill, not strength
gains.4: A lifetime self-defense skill for you and your family

lesson2.closing: Danger comes without warning. Wing Chun Self-Defense 9 Forms – simple, real, effective. Stay safe, stay confident.

program.title: Build your own Tai Chi & traditional arts practice, step by step
program.subtitle: This Program page connects all courses into one clear path: start with foundations, then grow into Qigong, Tai Chi, Wing Chun, TCM thinking and Feng Shui. You can follow the suggested order or choose the path that fits your current life.
program.ideal1: You prefer a guided sequence instead of "start anywhere you like".
program.ideal2: You have 10–30 minutes a day and want to make it count.
program.ideal3: You want both physical practice and cultural understanding.

level1.label: Level 1 · Foundations
level1.title: Posture, breathing & basic Tai Chi
level1.desc: Focus on safety and consistency. You'll start with standing, breathing and simple, gentle movements to build stability.
level1.point2: Tai Chi Mountain Practice · Part 1: stance, steps, basic hand shapes
level1.point3: 10–15 minutes daily, feeling breath and body weight changes

level2.label: Level 2 · Build a habit
level2.title: 8-week balance & wellness plan
level2.desc: Once you can follow the basics, move into an 8-week plan combining Tai Chi, Qigong and gentle face relaxation.
level2.point1: 3 days/week: Tai Chi Mountain + standing practice
level2.point3: 1 day/week: TCM for daily life – body types & routines

level3.label: Level 3 · Deep dive & expansion
level3.title: Wing Chun, Feng Shui & culture
level3.desc: When your body feels stable, choose a more martial or more cultural focus to deepen your journey.
level3.point1: Wing Chun Foundations for structure & partner drills
level3.point2: Feng Shui & Space Harmony for home and workspace
level3.point3: Combine with TCM for a body–energy–space perspective

program.exampleWeekDesc: Here is a very practical weekly schedule: 10–25 minutes per day, even with a busy job. You can copy it or adjust it to your life.
program.saturday: Wing Chun basics or Feng Shui

spaceFocus.title: If you start from space & lifestyle
spaceFocus.desc: Begin with Feng Shui & Space Harmony plus TCM for Daily Life: tidy your space and routines first, then add gentle Tai Chi and Qigong. This order is very friendly if you've been under stress and movement feels hard at the moment.

lesson1.title: Bone Vitality Qigong — Joint Renewal
lesson1.subtitle: Clear deep joint blockages and quickly relieve cold pain and stiffness in the shoulders, neck, waist and knees
lesson1.intro: Stop enduring joint pain! Don't rely on painkillers and massages that only treat the symptoms, not the root cause! Bone-Invigorating Health Qigong is taught personally by Master Liu Shigao, the 15th-generation authentic inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect. This 11-minute course is specially designed for stiff joints, cold and aching bone cavities, and sedentary strain. It unblocks stagnation deep in the joints, relieves pain in the shoulders, neck, lower back and knees, and restores flexibility to your joints.
lesson1.painPoint1: Office workers: Stiff and sore shoulders, neck and lower back, difficulty standing after sitting, limited range of motion when turning or bending
lesson1.painPoint2: Middle-aged and elderly people with joint discomfort: Knee pain, joint cracking, difficulty climbing stairs, worsened symptoms on rainy days
lesson1.painPoint3: People with minor sports injuries: Mild waist and knee strain, unable to do intense exercise, in need of gentle recovery
lesson1.painPoint4: People with cold-damp constitution: Cold and tight joints, cold hands and feet, persistent cold pain deep in the bones year-round
lesson1.instructorTitle: 15th-Generation Authentic Inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect
lesson1.instructorPoint1: Authentic Lineage: Disciple of Grandmaster Zhong Yunlong, the 14th-generation leader of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect
lesson1.instructorPoint2: Professional Achievements: Double champion in international Tai Chi and Tai Chi Sword competitions
lesson1.instructorPoint3: Precise Teaching: Trained systematically at the Wudang Zixiao Palace since childhood
lesson1.benefit1Desc: Qi penetrates the bone cavities, dissolves cold-damp stagnation, quickly relieves stabbing and cold pain in shoulders, waist and knees
lesson1.benefit2Desc: Stretches ligaments and joint capsules, improves range of motion, ends joint cracking and difficulty bending
lesson1.benefit3Desc: Draws Yang Qi inward, expels deep cold-damp from joints, reduces discomfort on rainy days
lesson1.benefit4Desc: Enhances muscle and ligament support, reduces joint wear, prevents recurring strain
lesson1.benefit5Desc: 11-minute short practice, no equipment needed, can be done at home or in the office
lesson1.closing: Stop enduring joint pain at a young age. Bone-Invigorating Health Qigong — 11 minutes to unblock, warm and strengthen your joints every day.

lesson2.title: Wuji Hunyuan Standing — Kidney Essence & Vital Energy
lesson2.subtitle: Gather qi and strengthen the foundation, replenish vital essence, relieve weakness, fatigue and cold extremities
lesson2.intro: Stop suffering from physical weakness, fatigue and sore waist and knees! Stop relying blindly on supplements that only make you weaker! Hunyuan Wuji Stance is taught personally by Master Liu Shigao, the 15th-generation authentic inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect. This 10-minute ancient standing practice is specially designed for those with insufficient kidney Qi, depleted primordial energy, cold hands and feet, and poor concentration. It gathers Qi and consolidates your foundation, replenishing your exhausted body from the root.
lesson2.painPoint1: People with insufficient kidney Qi: Sore, weak waist and knees, low energy
lesson2.painPoint2: People with cold constitution: Cold hands and feet, cold lower abdomen, aversion to wind and cold
lesson2.painPoint3: People with high stress and late nights: Fatigue, poor concentration, easy palpitations
lesson2.painPoint4: People with insomnia and restless sleep: Difficulty falling asleep, light sleep, easy waking
lesson2.painPoint5: People with weak immunity: Prone to colds and frequent minor illnesses
lesson2.instructorTitle: 15th-Generation Authentic Inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect
lesson2.instructorPoint1: Authentic Lineage: Bestowed the Taoist name "Shigao" — core 15th-generation inheritor, decades of Wudang internal stance practice
lesson2.instructorPoint2: Professional Achievements: Double champion in international Tai Chi and Tai Chi Sword competitions
lesson2.instructorPoint3: Precise Teaching: Proficient in the essence of "gathering Qi through standing and returning to origin"
lesson2.benefit1Title: Strengthens kidneys and consolidates primordial Qi
lesson2.benefit1Desc: Quickly gathers scattered vital energy, relieves soreness and fatigue
lesson2.benefit2Desc: Improves circulation in the lower body, warms hands, feet and abdomen from within
lesson2.benefit3Title: Calms the mind and stabilizes emotions
lesson2.benefit3Desc: Relieves irritation, calms the heart, deepens sleep
lesson2.benefit4Desc: Keeps you alert and focused during the day
lesson2.benefit5Title: Zero-threshold and easy to stick to
lesson2.benefit5Desc: 10-minute standing practice, no space or equipment required, suitable for all ages
lesson2.closing: Just 10 minutes a day to gradually recover your scattered vital energy. Hunyuan Wuji Stance — simple, deep, effective.

lesson3.title: Wudang Eight Brocades — Spleen & Digestion Care
lesson3.subtitle: Regulate the spleen and stomach, strengthen digestion and absorption, and accelerate metabolism
lesson3.intro: Stop suffering from weak spleen and stomach, indigestion, bloating and edema! Wudang Taoist Baduanjin is taught personally by Master Liu Shigao, the 15th-generation authentic inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect. This 17-minute ancient Daoyin practice specially regulates the spleen and stomach, enhances digestion, improves absorption and speeds up metabolism, solving the root problems of poor appetite, malabsorption, weight gain and weakness.

lesson3.painPoint2: People with intestinal disorders: Recurring constipation or loose stools, abdominal discomfort
lesson3.painPoint3: People with bloating and edema: Slow metabolism, fat accumulation, heavy body
lesson3.painPoint4: People with sallow complexion: Malnutrition, poor absorption, fatigue and laziness

lesson3.benefit3Desc: Reduces bloating and fat, improves circulation, reduces excess fat and edema

lesson4.title: Wudang Sanfeng Tai Chi 18 — Heart Calm & Restful Sleep
lesson4.subtitle: Calm the spirit and nourish the heart, relieve stress, improve sleep and soothe emotions
lesson4.intro: Stop being destroyed by insomnia, palpitations, irritation and internal friction! Tai Chi for Heart Calming is taught personally by Master Liu Shigao, the 15th-generation authentic inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect. This 11-minute gentle Tai Chi set specially calms the mind, relieves stress, improves sleep and soothes emotions, helping you relax mentally and physically.

lesson4.painPoint2: Anxious and stressed people: Irritability, overthinking, mental tension

lesson5.subtitle: Soothe liver qi, release stagnation, and clear liver fire

Tai Chi for Soothing the Liver is taught personally by Master Liu Shigao, the 15th-generation authentic inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect. This 11-minute stretching Tai Chi set specially soothes the liver, regulates Qi, releases stagnation and clears liver fire, making your body free of blockage, tightness and irritation.

lesson6.subtitle: Moisten and nourish the lungs, strengthen lung qi, boost immunity and relieve dry throat
lesson6.intro: Stop catching colds every season and feeling uncomfortable in the wind! Tai Chi for Nourishing the Lungs is taught personally by Master Liu Shigao, the 15th-generation authentic inheritor of the Wudang Sanfeng Sect. This 28-minute breath-coordinated Tai Chi set specially moistens the lungs, strengthens lung Qi, improves immunity and relieves dry throat, making you full of energy with stronger defense.
